name: Public quote eyebrow falls back to YOUR CONTRACTOR when the contractor has no name set
description: Tests the public-quote shell's brand fallback chain: contractor.businessName → contractor.name → 'Your contractor' (rendered uppercase via CSS). Once P1.1 onboarding captures a name, the same public link automatically re-brands without any FE change because the BE projection already reads UserStore + BusinessIdentityStore.

persona.name: Asha Patel (customer)
persona.role: Receiving a quote from a contractor who hasn't onboarded yet
persona.context: Got the quote email; opens it on her phone.

preconditions: Contractor account: user.name === undefined; businessIdentity.businessName === undefined

steps.expect: Eyebrow at top of the card reads 'YOUR CONTRACTOR' (uppercase via text-transform); contact-back footer reads 'Questions? Reach your contractor: <phone>' because contractor.name is unset

steps.action: (Contractor) on /assistant replies to the onboarding ask: 'Diego, Riley Roofing Co.'

steps.expect: Eyebrow now reads 'RILEY ROOFING CO.' (uppercase); contact-back reads 'Questions? Reach Diego: <phone>' — no FE deploy required

evaluationCriteria.check: Eyebrow uppercase styling is consistent regardless of source string; long business names wrap or truncate gracefully (max-width: 640px container)
Customer never sees PAPERWORK MONSTERS as the brand — that violates the 'whitelabel-feel' goal of the public surface
BE projection in public-controller never includes contractor.userId or other internal fields; tests assert 'userId' NOT in pub
Eyebrow is decorative copy; the actual h1 is the quote summary, which screen readers announce first
